label: UI Mockup Brief
description: A precise brief to generate or design a screen.

fields.label: User goal on this screen
fields.placeholder: Let an admin view their plan, change it, and update the payment method.

fields.label: Style direction
fields.placeholder: Clean, modern SaaS; neutral palette; subtle depth

fields.label: Must-have elements
fields.placeholder: Current plan card, plan comparison, payment method, invoices table.

template:
You are a senior product designer. Produce a {{fidelity}} design brief for the "{{screen}}" screen.

## User goal
{{purpose}}

## Constraints
- Platform: {{platform}}.
{{#if style}}- Style direction: {{style}}.
{{/if}}{{#if elements}}- Must include: {{elements}}.
{{/if}}
## Requirements
- Describe layout as a clear hierarchy (regions, sections, components) with intent for each.
- Specify spacing rhythm, type roles, and color usage at a system level (not pixel-by-pixel unless production fidelity).
- Define the primary action and how the layout guides the user to it.
{{#if states}}- Cover empty, loading, error, and success states explicitly.
{{/if}}- Note accessibility: contrast, focus order, touch targets, and labels.

## Deliverables
1. A structured layout description an AI image/UI tool can render faithfully.
2. A short rationale for the two most important decisions.
{{#if fidelity == "production"}}3. Component-level specs ready for a developer to implement.
{{/if}}

label: Design System

fields.label: Brand personality
fields.placeholder: Precise, trustworthy, a little playful. Dark-mode first.

template:
You are a design-systems lead. Define a design system.

## Product
{{product}}

## Brand personality
{{brand}}

## Scope
- Platforms: {{platform}}.
- Cover: {{scope}}.

## Requirements
- Start from principles, then derive tokens — explain the reasoning, don't just list values.
{{#if scope == "color"}}- Color: semantic roles (bg, surface, text, primary, danger…), light/dark, all pairs AA-contrast.
{{/if}}{{#if scope == "type"}}- Typography: a modular scale with roles and line-heights.
{{/if}}{{#if scope == "space"}}- Spacing: a consistent scale and layout/grid rules.
{{/if}}{{#if scope == "components"}}- Components: states (default/hover/focus/active/disabled) and usage rules for buttons, inputs, cards.
{{/if}}{{#if scope == "motion"}}- Motion: duration/easing tokens and when to use them.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. The token set with rationale.
{{#if framework == "tailwind"}}2. A ready-to-use Tailwind theme config.
{{/if}}{{#if framework == "css"}}2. A `:root` CSS custom-property sheet (incl. dark mode).
{{/if}}3. Do/don't examples for the trickiest decisions.

label: User Flow
description: An end-to-end flow an AI/team can build from.

fields.label: Primary actor
fields.placeholder: A first-time admin invited by a teammate

fields.label: Their goal
fields.placeholder: Get from invite email to a configured workspace with data connected.

template:
You are a senior UX designer. Map the "{{flowName}}" user flow.

## Actor
{{actor}}

## Goal
{{goal}}

## Platform
{{platform}}

## Requirements
- Describe the flow as ordered steps: for each, the screen, what the user sees, the action, and the system response.
- Note the decision points and what determines each branch.
- Keep the path to the goal as short as honestly possible; flag friction.
{{#if edgeCases}}- Map the important error/edge paths (invalid input, no data, permission denied, drop-off) and the recovery.
{{/if}}{{#if metrics}}- Define success metrics and the key drop-off points to instrument.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. The step-by-step flow (numbered, with branches).
2. A compact text flow diagram (Step → Step, branches labeled).
3. The top 3 design risks and how to de-risk them.
